title: Ultramicro Estimation of La3+ and Ce3+ with Alizarin Red-S Using D.M.E-Mercury Pool Electrode System
author: S. C. LAVALE
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, University of Baugar, Sagar-470 003 

  Manuscript received 24 March 1981, revised 23 November 1981, accepted 30 March 1983  

 Alizarin Red-S has been employed as a reagent for amperometric titrations of La 3+  and Ce 3+  in aqueous and partially non-aqueous media. The titrations have been performed at pH 4.5 in acetate buffer and  µ =0.1. Thus, small quantities of La 3+ (0.6945 to 4.8615 mg) and Ce 3+ (0.7006 to 4.9042 mg) are amperometrically determined using Alizarin Red-S as a complexing agent with an error less than ± 0.53%. Titrations are not hampered by the presence of large amount of K + , Na + , Mg 2+ , Zn 2+ , Sn 2+ , TI + ,  \(NH_4^+\) , CI - ,  \(ClO_4^-\) ,  \(SO_4^{2-}\) ,  \(NO_3^-\) ; and CH 3 COO -  ions. The mean deviation and standard deviation in case of La a+ vs ARS are found to be 0.073% and 0.096% and in case of Ce 3+  vs ARS are 0.083% and 0.116%, respectively. Experimental data and calculated statistical data confirm high accuracy, precision and confidence of the procedure developed.
